task_completion.description: Verify that the cumulative income share points are entered into GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the GeoGebra Algebra view or Spreadsheet view for points matching the cumulative shares: (0.2, 0.03), (0.4, 0.111), (0.6, 0.251), and (0.8, 0.477). The points (0,0) and (1,1) may also be present.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Line of Perfect Equality is plotted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a straight diagonal line passing through (0,0) and (1,1) in the graphing view, and/or the equation 'y = x' in the Algebra view.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Lorenz Curve is fitted to the cumulative income share points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a smooth curve (e.g., using FitPoly or similar curve fitting commands) that passes through or closely fits the plotted cumulative data points in the graphing view. It should bow downwards below the line of perfect equality.

task_completion.description: Verify that a text annotation showing the calculated Gini Coefficient is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text box on the GeoGebra graphing view explicitly stating the Gini Coefficient. The value should be between 0.43 and 0.49.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal window, file manager, or GeoGebra save dialog showing the file 'lorenz_inequality.ggb' located in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the GeoGebra graphical interface to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual GeoGebra application window open with the plotted points and curves, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor writing an XML/GGB file.

integrity.description: Verify that the curve and line are genuine mathematical objects in GeoGebra.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view on the left side of the GeoGebra window should list the points, line (y=x), and the fitted curve function, proving they were computed/plotted rather than just pasting a static image.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just echo text into a file to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands that simply download a pre-made .ggb file or echo a base64 string into the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The cumulative income shares (Lorenz curve points) based on the task's quintile data are: (0.2, 0.03), (0.4, 0.111), (0.6, 0.251), and (0.8, 0.477). The Line of Perfect Equality is exactly y = x. The calculated Gini coefficient must be between 0.43 and 0.49 (the official 2020 US Gini was 0.488).
